About Me

I am broadly interested in using physics and computation to study chemical systems.

I am an undergraduate at the University of Maryland, where I study Biochemistry and Computer Science. I am currently researching molecular simulations with Dr. Pratyush Tiwary. I have previously worked with Dr. Jeffrey Smith at the National Institutes of Health to develop simulations of networks of biological neurons.

For a more detailed description of my projects, you can take a look at my CV here, or feel free to send me an email at pravin4094@gmail.com

Outside of research, I used to speedsolve Rubik’s cubes, and I am an aspiring runner.
